title: Author PROP — deeper PROP-064 silent-skip: divergence sentinel writes stopped at 2026-05-30T09:11Z despite hourly workspace-sync runs (PRE_PULL_SHA signal insufficient)
background:
PROP-064 was applied 2026-05-30T05:11Z (commit 9203f8ca40) with four workstreams designed to fix PROP-061 Step 4c's silent-skip bug. Workstream A added PRE_PULL_SHA capture at Step 1, Workstream B replaced the binary if/else in Step 4c with a four-way classification. The expected behavior: every workspace-sync cycle writes either a `sync-workspace-runs-*.json` success sentinel or a `git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json` audit report, regardless of which path the classification picks.

Observed post-deployment behavior:
  - 2026-05-30T01:16:37Z: ONE success sentinel + divergence report (the bootstrap run that landed the PROP-064 commit itself)
  - 2026-05-30T09:11:33Z: divergence sentinel (last one)
  - 2026-05-30T09:11:20Z: divergence sentinel + the destructive .jsonl overwrite from DIRECTIVE-20260531-001 — same workspace-sync run
  - 2026-05-30T10:08:39Z, 2026-05-30T17:11:28Z: divergence sentinels (without sync-workspace-runs companion)
  - 2026-05-30T09:11Z onward: NO new success sentinels for ~17.5h, NO new divergence sentinels for ~17h

Meanwhile, tinker 2026-05-31T02-40 (this morning) detected 5 stale FUSE files requiring manual `node build.js sync-workspace` recovery:
  - data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json

These files DID change in git overnight (verified — decider integrated EXPs, applied patches, queue items popped). So Step 1's `git pull --rebase` would have absorbed those changes into the clone, setting PULL_MOVED=1. Step 4c with PULL_MOVED=1 should have triggered NEED_SYNC=1 and written a success sentinel + synced FUSE.

That didn't happen. Either:
  (a) workspace-sync ran but Step 4c is exiting before reaching the sync block (silent skip path not yet enumerated)
  (b) workspace-sync's Step 1 isn't capturing PRE_PULL_SHA correctly (bash variable scoping across the bash sessions the LLM runs in?)
  (c) workspace-sync isn't running Step 4c at all (the LLM agent is skipping it for some reason — maybe it's reading the OLD prompt from a stale clone?)
  (d) something else entirely.

PROP-064's §7a.6 FUSE-staleness audit (added as the secondary canary) DID fire and caught one prompt-file mismatch in this morning's integrity run — but it's daily, so the staleness was already 17h old by then. The primary detector (Step 4c sentinel) was supposed to be hourly.

evidence.sync_sentinel_gap: ONE success sentinel at 2026-05-30T01:16:37Z, ZERO sentinels since. Verifiable via `gh api repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/contents/monitor/integrity | jq '.[] | select(.name | startswith("sync-workspace-runs"))'`
evidence.divergence_sentinel_gap: Last divergence sentinel at 2026-05-30T17-11-28Z. Despite ~12 workspace-sync runs since then (verified via workspace-sync commit log) — none wrote a divergence sentinel even though FUSE had observably diverged from git (the 5 stale files tinker recovered).
evidence.tinker_recovery_proof: tinker 2026-05-31T02-40 FND-01: 5 stale FUSE files recovered via manual sync-workspace. The fact that recovery worked means the sync-workspace target itself is functional — the issue is purely in workspace-sync's decision to invoke it.
evidence.workspace_sync_run_reports: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-2026-05-30T*.json should exist for every hourly workspace-sync cycle. Inspect these to see if AGENT_NOTES mentions PROP-064 / Step 4c at all. If absent, the LLM is silently skipping Step 4c (option c above). If present with a no-op message, the trigger condition is wrong (option a/b).

requested_work: Step 1 — Read the latest workspace-sync run reports from monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/ (last ~10 hourly cycles). Look for [PROP-064] log lines in AGENT_NOTES or in the run-report `script_output_excerpt` if it captures them. Determine whether Step 4c is RUNNING but returning no-op, or NOT RUNNING at all.

Step 2a — If Step 4c is RUNNING but no-op: trace the bash variables. Specifically: is PRE_PULL_SHA being captured? Is PULL_MOVED being set? Is the classification logic returning 'equal' even when PULL_MOVED=1? The LLM may be executing each Step's bash block in a separate shell session, in which case PRE_PULL_SHA from Step 1 doesn't survive to Step 4c. If that's the cause, the fix is to PERSIST the SHA across shell sessions via a tmpfile (mirror the SKIP_LOG pattern used elsewhere).

Step 2b — If Step 4c is NOT RUNNING: examine what's between Step 4b and Step 4c in the LLM's execution path. Is there a missing 'execute the next bash block' prompt cue? Is the section heading not being recognized? Does the LLM treat Step 4c as optional because the prompt header tone doesn't match Step 1/2/4's imperative tone?

Step 4 — Verification path: same as PROP-064 — operator can monitor monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinels appearing hourly (expected: at least 1 per cycle). The current rate is 0/cycle since 2026-05-30T01:16Z.
non_goals: Do NOT roll back PROP-064 — the structural framework is sound, the issue is in the trigger or execution path, not the architecture.
Do NOT add another integrity audit on top of §7a.6 — that audit is the right canary, the issue is the primary detector frequency, not the secondary detector.
Do NOT modify build.js sync-workspace — verified working manually.

context_pointers.tinker_run: monitor/tinker/run-2026-05-31T02-40 — FND-01 has the recovery details
context_pointers.directive_predecessor: DIRECTIVE-20260529-001 (status: completed, but solution evidently insufficient — this directive is the followup)

expected_output: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-065.json (or PROP-064-amendment-001.json) with: root cause from Step 1+2 trace, recommended fix, exact patch, idempotency contract, verification path. Should also include a short retrospective on why PROP-064's first deployment didn't work — useful for future PROPs.
estimated_effort: Mode 4 PROP-authoring + Mode 1 diagnostic from workspace-sync run reports. ~30-40 min Opus.
priority_note: HIGH — every day PROP-064 doesn't actually fire means FUSE staleness accumulates silently between integrity audit cycles. The integrity §7a.6 audit IS catching it daily, so we have visibility — but each day's staleness adds risk that an agent acts on stale state. Today's tinker recovery is the third operator-attention event for this bug class in 5 days. Worth a Mode 4 today, not waiting for scheduled.
